body_txt: A character hitches a ride by clinging to the underside of a vehicle, often a big truck with high ground clearance. This can be done as a means of tailing someone who has their own vehicle when you do not and you cannot risk losing them, or as a means of infiltrating (or exfiltrating) an enemy compound, dropping off the vehicle once you are through the gates. The character may also cling to the bottom of a moving stagecoach, cart or train. In science fiction, it may be done with an aerocar. Do Not Try This at Home . There are a lot of moving parts underneath a vehicle that can chew up a human body real good. As well, some parts get hot enough to burn you. And when you fall off — not if, when — not only will you hit the road at high speed, you stand a good chance of being run over by the very vehicle you were riding on, or the one coming behind. And if you somehow maintain your grip, you'll be easily dragged to death against the pavement. Compare Outside Ride . When a vehicle isn't involved, you may have a case of Container Cling .
